# UC-13.6.13 · Grafana Alerting Notification Channel Delivery Failures

## Description

When Slack webhooks or PagerDuty routing keys break, Grafana fires alerts that never arrive—worse than noisy paging because teams assume silence means health. Channel-level failure rates isolate broken integrations quickly.

## Value

Restores confidence that Grafana-native alerting paths actually reach on-call tools.

## Implementation

Normalize `integration_type` (`slack`, `pagerduty`, `teams`). Deduplicate on `fingerprint` if present. Create a dead-letter index for raw payloads with secrets redacted.

## SPL

```spl
index=observability sourcetype="grafana:alerting"
| search "notification" OR contact_point OR contactPoint
| eval failed=if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)failed to send|delivery failed|retry|429|503|invalid webhook"),1,0)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as attempts, sum(failed) as failures by _time, contact_point, integration_type
| eval fail_rate=round(100*failures/nullif(attempts,0),2)
| where failures > 0 AND fail_rate > 1
| sort -failures
```

## Visualization

Line chart (fail_rate by channel), pie of integration types failing, table with last error snippet.

## References

- [Grafana — Alerting notifications](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana/latest/alerting/configure-notifications/)
